phrase: add a built-in tool get_pending_user_messages which would return the pending user messages and clear it. also, just before submitting the results of the tool calls to the llm, check if there are pending user messages - and if there are - insert the synthetic tool call into the tool_calls array in that turn, and the corresponding tool call result, and clear the pending messages array. Also, when returning a normal assistant reply, before yielding, check if the user message can be populated from pending messages, and if so - then take it from there and clear that accumulator before kicking off the turn.

need to have up/down to move one line up and one line down in the multiline editor input, even if these multilines are due to wrapping of the editor. no need to have surgical precision but just to be able to move visually up and down. If the editor is single line only - the up and down should function as history prev/next navigation.

add a display of the pending messages that would show just above the editor, so the user has the indication when the message gets drained ? (and when it gets drained, it should be inserted into the tty stream)
implement the "todo" tools
display the edit_file arguments in a form of a nicely formatted diff, and the same for plan_edits
make the sandbox possible to turn off via config and/or user input /sandbox on|off
pending messages should also trigger not only when the user message arrives during active tool calls, but also during the active LLM inference call
pending messages is 'off by one': first message just displays "pending messages", the second pending message makes the first pending message show under the "pending messages" title, etc.
implement a standalone llm-call actor, which would accept the provider id + instruction string and return a string of the result.

TUI: show an activity indicator (spinner) when there is an active LLM request in flight, so the user knows the agent is working and not frozen. Use braille spinner frames (like opencode: ⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏ at ~80ms) with a '⋯' fallback when animations are disabled. The agent already publishes LlmStream/LlmThinking events on the stream topic — drive the spinner off those (start on send_conversation, stop on LlmStreamEnd). Also show 'Calling tool: <name>' with the spinner during tool execution.

TUI: collapse long tool results instead of blasting them fully on screen. Show first ~3 lines (or ~10 for bash) then '…' with click-to-expand. See tmp/opencode/packages/tui/src/util/collapse-tool-output.ts for the reference algorithm (pure function: split by newlines, slice maxLines, append ellipsis, track overflow bool). Currently gladiator dumps the full tool result text into the chat stream which floods the display on large grep/cat outputs.

Stuck-model detection: if an LLM request has been streaming for longer than a threshold (e.g. 90s with no new chunk, or total thinking > 3 min), interrupt it (the control topic interrupt mechanism already exists), take the accumulated reasoning text, send it to the standalone llm-call actor with a prompt like 'Extract the key insights, decisions, and next actions from this thinking trace as a concise summary', then inject the summary back as a user message: "Here is the useful information spotted from your thinking — see if it helps you progress." This turns infinite-think loops into productive pivots. Depends on the standalone llm-call actor fixme.

Graceful max-iterations recovery: when max_iterations is reached, instead of just stopping with a warning, the agent should (a) summarize what it accomplished so far and what remains, (b) save a handoff note via restart_from_file or at least record a conclusion, and (c) optionally auto-restart with a 'continue from where you left off' instruction. Currently it just prints 'Max iterations (50) reached' and goes idle, losing all momentum. Observed in supervisor session: gladiator spent all 50 iterations reading files and never wrote any code — the recovery should detect this exploration-only pattern and nudge toward action.

Add WebFetch and WebSearch tools. WebFetch: takes a URL + optional format (markdown/text/html), fetches it, converts to requested format, returns content (summarize if very large). WebSearch: takes a query, returns results. Look at how opencode (tmp/opencode) and open-strix (tmp/open-strix) implement these — opencode has a webfetch tool in its tool registry. Pick a dependency or hand-roll with reqwest (already in the workspace) + a simple HTML-to-markdown converter. Register as built-in tools behind config toggles. The agent currently cannot look up docs/APIs online, which causes it to guess at crate APIs (observed: stuck guessing the similar crate API in a loop).
we need to show in the TUI the current context taken up by the text. obviously it will need to reset whenever the session is being restarted from file.
need to add a WebFetch tool - see how opencode has implemented it (./tmp/opencode)
the interrupt by esc does not stop the spinner in the bottom line - this should be fixed
in the tool call output inside the TUI - we can replace the "[tool] ..." text by the "Calling tool .." text since the successful parsing of the former always results in the latter.

the "Working..." indicator starts showing only when the LLM starts outputting its content, wrongly resulting in the 'gladiator ready' during the message prefill phase, which can be long. Maybe that phase needs to be some different busy message, but with timer/progress spinner as well.
have the edit_file/plan_edits show the full diffs, in color - and not hide them.

The spinner needs its own state when the agent is in the state of issuing tool calls - in case the tool calls take much time. it should probably show total tool calls, and number of completed tool calls during that moment.

the "N chars" indicator on the left needs to be shown/populated also when we are parsing/forming the tool calls - maybe with the respective different title instead of simply "Working..." - for that particular state.
the progressive writing of [tool] messages in the chat should show linefeeds, etc. - it will be replaced later but transiently it is nice to see the progress.

For "Thinking ..." indicator - it might be a nice idea to display the anticipated time left, based on the previous occurrences - take the amount of "new" information in the past requests divided by the time it took to process it - multiplied by the amount of "new" information in this request. If that estimate turns out to be very wrong - assume the cache was off and consider the entire request to be "new" and give estimation based on that. This would be a nice quality of life improvement for the UX

a tweak: if the user has been using up/down to check history, interpret the up/down as history controls even in multiline messages, until any other navigation is done within the multiline - then start treating up/down as the line navigation.

when there are pending messages shown in the TUI, it would be great to be able to "retract them to edit" upon pressing of up arrow in the TUI - investigate if that is something doable - press up arrow and retract all of the pending messages as a single one for editing, and clear the pending messages list, and then when done editing, resubmit the message.
[i] Sending request to LLM... is probably redundant

Ctrl-K at the end of the line in the multiline input case should remove the newline after the cursor - that is, append the next line to the current line, after the cursor position - without changing the curos position.

the rendering of tool+tool_result really need improvement for visuals - there should be detection of what kind of tool it is, for edits it should show the diff with colors, also there is no point to duplicate the tool name across both - i.e. it should be a single coherent block in the log. If we have structured elements in the chat scroll - maybe create a new structured element if that is easier to handle.
read tmp/editing.txt and implement a worksspace member mcp-tagged-fileops which would implement the ideas from there.

rename_symbol tool does naive string substitution (corrupts comments and declarations, leaves actual symbol references unchanged) instead of applying LSP WorkspaceEdit edits as syntactic ranges per occurrence. Needs to parse the rename edit's textEdits and apply each by range, not by find/replace on the whole file. See mcp-rlsp/src/tools/refactoring.rs (rename_symbol_impl).

The estimate for prefill ("Thinking..." phase) should use precise time intervals (milliseconds?) and have a better prediction model - the speed generatlly degrades with size, so it should keep a set of past samples, and use that to predict the polynomial of, say 5th degree, which would be used to predict the prefill time based on the current prefill size.

organize_imports tool fails with "No organize-imports code action available" on mcp-rlsp/src/main.rs even though imports are out of order. Root cause likely: (1) create_code_action_params passes context.only=["source.organizeImports"] but rust-analyzer may not advertise that literal, or (2) the full-range computation uses line_count via .lines().count() which counts lines without trailing newline correctly but end char u32::MAX is invalid for RA. See mcp-rlsp/src/analyzer/client.rs organize_imports() and create_code_action_params in protocol.rs. Debug by capturing raw codeAction response JSON from RA to see what kinds it returns, then match against source.organizeImports or source.sortMembers.

get_type_hierarchy tool always returns "No type hierarchy at <file:line:char>" for structs with impl blocks (tested on RustMcpServer). Root cause likely in prepareTypeHierarchy response handling — client.rs get_type_hierarchy() checks `response.get("result").as_array()` but RA may return null or the cursor position isn't on a valid type symbol. Also possible: did_open was called but indexing not complete before prepare request fired (no wait for diagnostics/publishDiagnostics). See mcp-rlsp/src/analyzer/client.rs get_type_hierarchy() and prepare_type_hierarchy_params in protocol.rs. Fix: add retry/wait after didOpen, verify cursor is on a struct/enum/trait keyword or type name, log raw RA response to diagnose empty result array vs null.

find_definition returns "No definition found" when called from a use-site whose def lives in another file (tested on `RustMcpServer` at main.rs:5 — RA returned empty result[]). Works fine for same-file defs. Root cause likely: rust-analyzer needs the target file to be opened via didOpen before it can resolve cross-file definitions, but find_definition_impl does NOT call did_open first (unlike get_diagnostics/extract_function which do). See mcp-rlsp/src/tools/analysis.rs find_definition_impl and client.rs find_definition(). Fix: add self.did_open(&canonicalize(file_path)).await? before sending textDocument/definition.

extract_function and inline_function tools are untested end-to-end. Both use codeAction requests via execute_code_action which calls apply_workspace_edit — same path as rename_symbol that had the pos_to_index off-by-one bug (now fixed). Need integration tests similar to apply_workspace_edit_renames_all_occurrences_correctly but for extract/inline scenarios, OR manual verification after MCP server restart. See mcp-rlsp/src/analyzer/client.rs extract_function() and inline_function(). Also: execute_code_action has a fallback path that calls workspace/executeCommand — verify RA actually supports command-based code actions or if only edit-based ones work.

MCP server child processes are spawned once at gladiator startup via McpServerRunner::spawn in gladiator-tools/src/mcp.rs and never respawned. If a child crashes or is killed, all its tools fail permanently until full gladiator restart. Also: rebuilding an MCP binary (e.g., cargo build --release) does NOT pick up changes because the old process holds stale code in memory — observed with mcp-rlsp PID 77524 still running buggy pos_to_index after fix was compiled. Need: (1) health-check / liveness detection on child stdio, (2) auto-respawn with same config + re-discover tools when death detected, (3) optional hot-reload trigger (e.g., SIGHUP or file-watch on binary path). The 10s timeout in McpTool::execute should also retry once after reconnect. See gladiator-tools/src/mcp.rs lines ~187-260 (McpServerRunner/spawn/shutdown/Handle/Drop).
store the input edit history across launches by maintaining ./tmp/history.json

RA returns null for ALL LSP queries on this workspace — find_definition, documentSymbol, hover, workspace/symbol all return result:null. Works fine on standalone temp projects and minimal 1-crate workspaces in /tmp. Root cause is project-specific: possibly the virtual manifest with "." as a member alongside path-dependency subcrates confuses RA's project discovery (cargo metadata succeeds but RA never loads source files). Tried: workspaceFolders, linkedProjects via initializationOptions, rootPath+rootUri combo, 120s wait — all still null. Next approach to try: generate rust-project.json manually OR remove "." from workspace members and see if that fixes it. See mcp-rlsp/tests/lsp_integration_test.rs for diagnostic tests."
vendor the ./tmp/open-strix-rust/mcp-pty-server
vendor the ./tmp/open-strix-rust/mcp-loader
vendor the ./tmp/open-strix-rust/mcp-pdf-server

Pasting the text results in a very very slow character-by-character insertion; we should improve the performance of that - and maybe treat the large amount of text somehow specially, without per-char screen redraw ?
add command to show what is going on with the ptys - should it be universal /mcp call ... command with different names of servers and tab completion for functions/arguments, and "?" help ?
please implement outgoing request doctoring as per tmp/outgoing-request-doctoring.md

As part of the context refresh, agent also needs to write a file surprises.md, which would contain all the surprizes it encountered in the session so far, and then perform the five-why analysis on them according to  tmp/five-whys.md

mcp-loader seems to have an issue with arguments: The tagged_fileops tools work but the MCP call interface seems to have argument passing issues — likely because gladiat or's `mcp_call` wraps args differently than direct JSON-RPC. could we take care of that ?

Could we have the result of [tool] entry and the tool call result entry be presented in aesthetically pleasing and readable way - like, a nice red-green diff in case it is an edit, or a command + its result if it was a command run ?
Could we have the counter of chars returned be seen in the UI similar to the state of "Working..." in the state where we are accumulating a tool call ?
add an internal tool to manipulate "context reminders": a warning thresholds, which, when crossed, would cause a one-time injection of the configurable message into the agent loop
add one-time and cron wake-ups: configurable messages that would be injected into the agent loop on schedule - also be able to inject those only if the loop is idle, and cancel otherwise.
add ability to use rust-analyzer on multiple projects at once, not just the main project root - or at least switch between the two. Have the tools to query active project(s) and to switch them.

If a user sends a pending message (while agent is busy) and then presses ESC-ESC to interrupt, the pending message remains stuck in pending_messages forever. The interrupt path should drain/clear pending messages so they do not accumulate and confuse the agent on the next turn.

cargo test should run all tests reliably. Current issues: (1) e2e_test has 2 #[ignore] tests that need --ignored to run — they should either be un-ignored if they work, or moved behind a feature gate. (2) mcp-rlsp integration tests hang indefinitely (they spawn RA which takes 300s) — they need a timeout or #[ignore] with a separate test runner. (3) 2 pre-existing TUI test failures (input_state_history_basic, input_state_history_no_duplicates_consecutive) need to be fixed or the tests updated to match current behavior. Goal: "cargo test --workspace" exits 0 in under 60s with no hangs.

design and implement the concept of subagents - the most frequent use case will be that the subagent is "called" with a specific task via an MCP tool; and the main agent waits for this subagent to finish before continuing (thus the subagent will show the output in the same chat window as the main agent, just with added "|" indentation. the subagent can in principle call nested subagent, etc, etc. the return value of subagent is the string with the result of the task.

for the estimation of the prefill ("Thinking...") phase - use a better estimation than linear - measure the previous prefills sizes + milliseconds taken, and then use that to derive polynomial 5th degree approximation which you use to then predict the time based on the anticipated prefill size.

Replace string-based tool call/result rendering with structured AppMessage fields. Currently bus_to_app_message flattens structured JSON ({function:{name,args}}) to a string, then render.rs string-matches (is_diff checks first line == "edit_file", is_bash checks for "[bash]" or "$" prefix) to decide formatting. This is fragile: a trailing space broke diff coloring (fixed in a77203e), parse_tool_result_id regex-parses formatted strings to recover the tool_call_id. Design: add optional structured fields to AppMessage (tool_name: Option<String>, tool_args: Option<Value>, tool_result: Option<String>, tool_kind: Option<ToolKind> enum {Edit, Bash, Read, Mcp, Other}). bus_to_app_message populates these from the structured bus payload. render.rs pattern-matches on tool_kind instead of string content. Eliminates all string-comparison fragility. Migration: keep content as fallback display text, add structured fields alongside, switch render.rs to prefer structured when present.
fix the broken tests - if they do not correspond to the reality anymore, adapt the tests to correspond to new reality, else if they highlight bugs - then fix the bugs
render todo_write tool result in a nicer way, rather than a lot of text
